sessions.note: 9 structurally distinct mask-and-OR spellings (decl orders, no-locals, named intermediates addr=a1&mask / hi+lo, operand commute, a1&=mask param reuse, tag_mask=~mask, mask=~tag_mask) compile to BYTE-IDENTICAL cc1 output, all distance 7. Mechanism (sched trace + lreg, tmp/oti*): sched1 try_split makes ADDR lui+ori, backward scheduler places the pair at block head (depth +1 from ori); post-sched1 live lengths ADDR=13 TAG=9; local-alloc qty pri floor_log2(3)*3/span -> TAG 3333 > ADDR 2307 -> TAG takes $a3, ADDR $t0 (target wants the reverse). Deterministic: same RTL in -> same alloc out. Flip requires >=4 pre-combine refs on the ADDR-mask pseudo (flow counts refs BEFORE combine) or span<9 -- no natural mask-and-OR spelling provides either; redundant-use respellings are forbidden coercion. The PsyQ P_TAG bitfield expansion provides the extra refs NATURALLY (expmed insert+extract both use 0xFFFFFF; combine folds the redundancy to identical bytes) -- that is WHY the rejected bitfield form hit 0. Resume = notes.md direction 3 (header-level OTag type, all OT funcs, user sign-off) -- a user decision, hence park.
